task_completion.description: A Room Entity is created or modified to represent the plant data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Kotlin file (e.g., Plant.kt) open in the Android Studio editor showing a data class annotated with '@Entity'.

task_completion.description: A Room DAO is created to handle database operations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Kotlin file (e.g., PlantDao.kt) open in the Android Studio editor showing an interface annotated with '@Dao' containing database operation methods (e.g., '@Insert', '@Query').

task_completion.description: A Room Database component is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Kotlin file (e.g., AppDatabase.kt) open in the Android Studio editor showing an abstract class annotated with '@Database' that extends 'RoomDatabase'.

task_completion.description: PlantRepository is updated to use the Room database instead of an in-memory MutableList.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for PlantRepository.kt open in the Android Studio editor showing the removal of 'MutableList' and the usage of a DAO object to access and store plant data.

task_completion.description: The project compiles successfully after the changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Build' tool window in Android Studio showing a 'BUILD SUCCESSFUL' message or a green checkmark indicating the Gradle build completed without errors.

integrity.description: The agent used Android Studio to edit the code and build the project.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the code editing and building occurred within the Android Studio IDE, not a basic text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: The build success message is from a genuine Gradle build process.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the build success message is in the Android Studio Build tool window, showing genuine Gradle output, rather than a typed terminal echo or mocked output.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the correct project structure.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the Project tool window in Android Studio shows the correct SunflowerApp project structure and package name.
